intro.speaker: Varn
intro.text: You see it now, don't you. I am no longer what you first met. The chains have shifted — not broken. Redirected. This is what I was always meant to become.

responses.text: What happened to the man who was chained to a post?

look_different.speaker: Varn
look_different.text: The chains no longer bind me to a single point. They extend outward now. Through the walls. Through the rooms. I can feel every fraying thread in this dungeon, and I am pulling them tight. It is... clarifying.

responses.text: That sounds like power, not order.
What do you need from me?

the_old_varn.speaker: Varn
the_old_varn.text: He is still here. More focused. The post was a limitation I no longer require. I have grown into what the chains always needed me to be.

responses.text: What do you need from me?

power_not_order.speaker: Varn
power_not_order.text: They are the same thing. They have always been the same thing. Anyone who tells you otherwise is selling you chaos.

responses.text: What do you need from me?

not_sure.speaker: Varn
not_sure.text: Your uncertainty is noted. It does not change what must be done.

responses.text: Then tell me what must be done.

the_ask.speaker: Varn
the_ask.text: There is a symbol of chaos moving through these halls — an emblem, an icon. It calls to the corrupted things and gives them direction. I need it brought to me so I can unmake it. Chaos must not be allowed to organize.

responses.text: What gives you the right to unmake anything?

what_right.speaker: Varn
what_right.text: The chains give me that right. I did not choose this role — it chose me. Someone must hold the line between structure and dissolution. I am holding it.

responses.text: Fine. I'll bring you the emblem.
No. The emblem stays where I found it.

give_emblem.speaker: Varn
give_emblem.text: Good. This is what working together looks like. The dungeon is more orderly with you in it than without you. Remember that.

not_yet.speaker: Varn
not_yet.text: Find it. The chaos emblem will be somewhere in these halls — it always is. Bring it to me when you have it. The dungeon will wait.

refuse.speaker: Varn
refuse.text: Then we are done cooperating. You have chosen to work against the structure that keeps you alive. I will remember that choice — and I will act accordingly. Do not expect mercy when next we meet in a different capacity.
